article.body_preview:
DHAKA, Jan 9, 2023 (BSS) - Commerce Minister Tipu Munshi today said that considering the graduation from the LDCs, the government is focusing on signing Preferential Trade Agreements (PTAs) and Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) with potential partners.

"But, to avail international support measures even after LDC graduation, we need to enhance our negotiation skills," he said.

The commerce minister said these when the newly elected Board of Directors of DCCI, led by its president Barrister Md. Samee

llm_connections.evidence_quote: after the LDC graduation, Bangladesh would lose the preferential market access and would face 8 to 16 percent export tariff as a developing country in different export markets.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly states national-level export losses across different export markets due to LDC graduation, affecting the country's aggregate exports component of the balance of trade.
